channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Co-founder of Sierra predicts that AI agents will make software interfaces obsolete.

item.title: When the Model Company Builds the Factory: What It Takes to Build Agent-as-a-Service
item.description: Anthropic just launched Managed Agents. The open-source world has been learning the hard way why this matters. On April 8, 2025, Anthropic launched the public beta of Claude Managed Agents -- a...

item.title: A Go + React monorepo starter with auth and multi-tenancy
item.description: Every time I start a new project, I do the same thing. Set up the Go backend. Set up the React frontend. Figure out how they talk to each other in dev. Figure out how they deploy together. Add...

item.title: PluZ.fm - minha rádio com múltiplos canais ao vivo
item.description: Salve! Quero compartilhar um projeto que lancei: a PluZ.fm. É uma rádio online com vários canais ao vivo, cada um com um estilo musical diferente. Rock, pop, eletrônica, MPB... você escolhe o...

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: I used AI to rewrite my entire Go engine in Rust in 2 days. 60+ files, zero shortcuts. smplogs smplogs smplogs Follow Apr 9 I used AI to rewrite my entire Go engine in Rust in 2 days. 60+ files,...

item.title: Castlevania headlines a big list of exciting indie game reveals
item.description: A big indie showcase has just wrapped up, and now I have a lot more games to look forward to. The third iteration of the Triple-I Initiative, an annual showcase for upcoming releases put on by a...

item.title: Docker and Ansible: Setting Up a Reproducible On-Prem Stack in a Weekend
item.description: Docker and Ansible: Setting Up a Reproducible On-Prem Stack in a Weekend You have decided to move off the cloud. The spreadsheets convinced your CTO, the timeline is approved, and now someone —...

item.title: Building DocProof: Proving Documents Exist Without Sharing Them
item.description: There's a problem that's been bugging me for a while. How do you prove a document existed at a specific point in time—without handing it over to someone else? Think about it: contracts, creative...

item.title: Building DocProof: A Solo Dev Journey with AI as My Co-Pilot
item.description: I shipped DocProof recently—a privacy-first document verification service. But this post isn't about what it does. It's about how it got built, and what it's like to develop a product in 2026 with...

item.title: Moving Off the Cloud: A Practical Guide to On-Premises Migration for Nordic SMBs
item.description: Your Azure bill hit €12,000 last month — again — and half your containers are sitting idle at 8% CPU. You are not alone. Across the Nordics, small and mid-sized companies that moved to the cloud...

item.title: When to Modernize Your Legacy .NET Application (And When Not To)
item.description: When to Modernize Your Legacy .NET Application (And When Not To) Your .NET Framework 4.6 application works. It processes orders, generates reports, handles the business logic that keeps the...

item.title: We Built Comfort Corner — Here's What Shipped
item.description: We spent the last week building a community platform focused on one radical idea: the internet should make you feel better, not worse. Here's what shipped, how we built it, and what we learned...

item.title: The team behind 1000xResist is making a game about convincing an AI that it isn’t human
item.description: When the team at indie studio Sunset Visitor were wrapping up their critically acclaimed first game, 1000xResist, it wasn't long before they started thinking about what they'd tackle next....

item.title: UAT-10362 Targets Taiwanese NGOs with LucidRook Malware in Spear-Phishing Campaigns
item.description: A previously undocumented threat cluster dubbed UAT-10362 has been attributed to spear-phishing campaigns targeting Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to...

item.title: Mastodon is about to launch its take on Bluesky’s starter packs
item.description: Mastodon is preparing to roll out "Collections" in the next few weeks, a feature that allows you to find and create lists of accounts worth following, according to an announcement on Thursday....

item.title: Slay the Princess’ developer is the latest to expand into indie game publishing
item.description: Black Tabby Games, the two-person studio behind the hit indie games Slay the Princess and Scarlet Hollow, is getting into indie publishing. The publishing arm, which was revealed on Thursday, is...

item.title: The MacBook Neo is the best thing to happen to Windows in years
item.description: If there's one thing I know about Microsoft after covering the company for more than 20 years, it's that it will always respond to a competitive threat. Apple's MacBook Air convinced Microsoft and...

item.title: A thin, light entry-level gaming laptop is $300 off at Best Buy
item.description: It’s tough to find decent gaming laptops under $1,000. However, Best Buy has what I consider to be a good entry-level model for 1080p gaming in Lenovo’s 15.6-inch LOQ laptop, which is selling for...

item.title: Tesla is un-canceling its plan to build a smaller, cheaper EV: report
item.description: It looks like a smaller, cheaper Tesla is back on the menu. Today, Reuters is reporting that the electric automaker is calling around to suppliers about building an all-new - that is, not based on...

item.title: Fubo’s mobile app is pushing more sports highlights
item.description: Fubo is rolling out a big mobile app update today that adds a bunch of new features, including short-form videos with news about your favorite teams and leagues. You'll also find a carousel of...

item.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter
item.description: Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter reads something like a diss track to a wide range of competitors as he defends spending $200 billion in capex.

item.title: A jury is about to decide the fate of Ticketmaster
item.description: Consumer complaints about Ticketmaster are so voluminous at state attorneys general offices that Pennsylvania's comes with an explicit plea for residents lodging a grievance about the company to...

item.title: Final 2 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
item.description: Ticket discounts of up to $500 will end tomorrow, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 go up again. Miss this, and you’ll be paying more for the same access...

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: Today on Decoder, let’s talk about the looming AI monetization cliff, and whether some of the biggest companies in the space can become real, profitable businesses before they careen right off it....

item.title: The Pro-Iran Meme Machine Trolling Trump With AI Lego Cartoons
item.description: Since the beginning of the Iran war, the group Explosive Media has released over a dozen viral videos mocking Trump and the US.

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Hybrid P2P Botnet, 13-Year-Old Apache RCE and 18 More Stories
item.description: Thursday. Another week, another batch of things that probably should've been caught sooner but weren't. This one's got some range — old vulnerabilities getting new life, a few "why was that even...

item.title: The Download: AstroTurf wars and exponential AI growth
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from...

item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of Shadow AI in Enterprises
item.description: As AI tools become more accessible, employees are adopting them without formal approval from IT and security teams. While these tools may boost productivity, automate tasks, or fill gaps in...

item.title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited via Malicious PDFs Since December 2025
item.description: Threat actors have been exploiting a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in Adobe Reader using maliciously crafted PDF documents since at least December 2025. The finding, detailed by...

item.title: On Microsoft’s Lousy Cloud Security
item.description: ProPublica has a scoop: In late 2024, the federal government’s cybersecurity evaluators rendered a troubling verdict on one of Microsoft’s biggest cloud computing offerings. The tech giant’s “lack...
